resources.string: Brass teeth grind, oiled by the red tithe.
BRASS GEARS TURN. BLOOD KEEPS THEM WET.

Peace is but the putrid gasp between hammer blows. Upon the ashen crust, brass hunger turns on a river of gore. Legions of iron march the world flat. Great engines graft steel into marrow and flesh into metal. Pillars of soot rise until the crimson vault forgets the stars. No throne is high enough to command it. No soul is small enough to be spared. The war engine reaps them both.
PEACE IS BREATH BETWEEN HAMMERS. BRASS GEARS TURN WITH BLOOD OIL. INFANTRY GRINDS THE GROUND. SMOKE HIDES THE STARS. WAR ENGINE FEEDS ON ALL.
The kiln of slaughter never cools.

The world is a sepulcher of soot, strangled by palls of black smoke. Iron-shod legions march in an eternal tide, pulverizing the soil into dust. The great mechanism churns, oiled by the vital fluids of the fallen. There is no conquest, only the campaign. The cosmic gears demand a tribute of strife, and we are but the coal for the fire.
GRAVEYARD OF ASH. BLACK SMOKE BLOTS SUN. ENDLESS HEAVY INFANTRY. MACHINE GRINDS ON. LUBRICATED BY BLOOD. NO VICTORY. ETERNAL CONFLICT. WE ARE FUEL.
